title: Control Points

content.question: Where are you flying blind without an alarm system?
sop: Identify the 3-5 metrics that matter most: cash reserve, response time, churn, a health marker.
Set a specific Threshold for each (see calculation, page 4), the number that means 'act now,' not just 'keep an eye on it.'
Use a tool or simple tracker that checks your Metric Value against that threshold automatically, not your memory.
Act immediately when a threshold is crossed, don't wait to see if it self-corrects.
Review thresholds quarterly; a number that never triggers may be set wrong, not actually safe.
questions: Where are you flying blind without an alarm system?
What number, if it crossed a specific threshold today, would you not even notice until it was already a crisis?
Which part of your business or life do you check only when something already feels wrong?
content.equation_example: Set a Threshold for your metric, e.g. cash reserve minimum $10,000. Current Metric Value: $7,500. Since $7,500 < $10,000, the alert triggers and corrective action starts. Above the threshold, no alert triggers, and no action is needed yet.
content.closing_statement: Set the alarm before you need it, not after.

caption:
Principle Nº 201: Control Points

Insert monitoring controllers.

Ask yourself: Where are you flying blind without an alarm system?
